message:
So last week we had a surprise outage, and I learned something funny about my family: we all panic in the group chat first, then *actually* panic.

Within seconds: "Is it just us?" "No power on Maple!" "MOM WHERE ARE THE FLASHLIGHTS" "I DON'T KNOW HONEY"

Turns out I had flashlights. My sister had a power bank. My brother's phone was already dead (naturally). And somehow we all survived without candles — which, let's be honest, was probably safer anyway.

But here's what stuck with me: we spent the first 10 minutes fumbling around when a quick little refresher on where we actually kept our stuff could've saved us some chaos. Now I've got a mental map. Flashlights labeled. Phone chargers in an actual place I remember.

It's not fancy. It's just... knowing. And having your people *know* too.

What's the weirdest place you've discovered a flashlight in your house?
